title: She Sauntered By The Swinging Seas
content:
She sauntered by the swinging seas,
    A jewel glittered at her ear,
    And, teasing her along, the breeze
    Brought many a rounded grace more near.

    So passing, one with wave and beam,
    She left for memory to caress
    A laughing thought, a golden gleam,
    A hint of hidden loveliness.

    1876
meaning:
William Ernest Henley's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "She Sauntered By The Swinging Seas"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
